title: blazewrit harness — decide step structured_output
description: Decide = the OWNER of the decision, sitting on Investigate output (every flow runs Decide, no skip). Output depth is declared via a per-flow MODE: record | plan | design. TARGET RUNTIME: grammar-constrained generation (M1 = SHAPE). This schema models the Decide OUTPUT as a top-level discriminated union on `result`: a normal `decision` (which itself carries the 3-mode record|plan|design sub-union), plus the two prose-mandated control outputs `reclassify_required` (triage_mismatch surfaced) and `request_upstream_deepen` (shallow Ground/Investigate, cap 1). $ref-s ./_defs.schema.json for all shared primitives (M4 single source). IMPORTANT: grammar here enforces SHAPE only. The MODE is FORCED MECHANICALLY by R6 from Investigate schema fields (NOT free-chosen by the LLM) — the orchestrator computes the forced mode and a SIBLING M2 validator_contract (see x-validator-contracts) asserts the emitted `mode` matches that R6 computation. Other truth-bearing fields (R6 mode-force, R16 chain, R20 verify probes in plans, R23 measured counts, R26 provenance, R36 citations, R5 cross-verify on Design) are likewise closed by deterministic code checks listed in x-validator-contracts, NOT by any keyword here.
$comment: NO root-level additionalProperties:false — JSON Schema additionalProperties only sees SIBLING properties, not properties introduced inside the oneOf branches. Closure is enforced per-branch (each oneOf member sets additionalProperties:false + lists its full property set). The root `properties.result` below is advisory (gives `result` a description); the binding discriminant + closure live in each branch.

result.$comment: Top-level discriminant. Grammar forces exactly one branch via the oneOf below.
result.description: Which Decide output this is. 'decision' = a real decision was made (carries `mode` sub-union). 'reclassify_required' = Investigate surfaced triage_mismatch; Decide does NOT proceed, signals reclassify (orchestrator re-enters Triage; cap 3/flow). 'request_upstream_deepen' = Ground/Investigate too shallow to decide; Decide signals upstream deepen (cap 1/flow, then Verify is final safety).

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH A — a real decision. Carries the 3-mode (record|plan|design) sub-union.

mode.$comment: R6 — the forced mode discriminant. Grammar enforces the value is one of the three AND (via the nested oneOf below) that the per-mode required fields are present. TRUTH (that this value == the R6-computed forced mode) is an M2 validator_contract, not a keyword.
mode.description: Decide mode = output depth. record (self-evident decision, depth=shallow) | plan (option selection, depth=medium) | design (full design, depth=deep). FORCED MECHANICALLY by R6 from Investigate fields — the orchestrator computes it; the LLM enters with it pre-forced. The nested oneOf on this same `mode` value forces exactly one per-mode payload.

decision_record.$comment: Reviewer rule: ALL modes require decision_record + reason. One-line authoritative statement of the decision taken.
decision_record.description: The decision itself, as one authoritative statement. REQUIRED in every mode (decide-reviewer: 'decision_record + reason 필수' for all modes).

reason.$comment: ALL modes require reason. The justification, grounded in Ground/Investigate facts.
reason.description: Why this decision — grounded in Ground/Investigate FACTS (reviewer: 'ground·investigate 사실에 근거'). REQUIRED in every mode. Carries provenance: source_tool names which Ground/Investigate origin the reason rests on (R26 floor).

grounded_in.description: RowRefs to the specific Ground/Investigate rows whose facts justify this decision (§5: Postgres row ids, never file paths).

based_on.$comment: §5 — based_on = RowRefs (Postgres rows), NEVER file paths. Legacy prose 'based_on: { investigate_ref, ground_ref }' translated to RowRef bundle.
based_on.description: Upstream rows this decision rests on. EVERY value is a RowRef (Postgres step_runs row id), never a file path. Decide always rests on Investigate and Ground; both are required here.

depth.$comment: Decide mode IS depth (Record=shallow / Plan/Design=deep per _defs.Depth). x-validator: depth must agree with mode.
depth.description: Adaptive Step Depth this Decide ran at. Decide maps mode→depth (Record=shallow; Plan/Design=deep). M2 validator asserts depth agrees with `mode`.

declared_next_step.$comment: R16 — producer DECLARES, orchestrator INJECTS expected_next_step. Chain match is M2 (folded into R27 validator).
declared_next_step.description: R16 advisory next step. Decide does NOT free-fill the authoritative successor; the orchestrator injects expected_next_step. M2 validator asserts declared_next_step == expected_next_step (chain divergence check). Carries the shared DeclaredNextStep shape.

unverified.$comment: R13 floor — KEEP polarity. A Decide claim the step could not itself verify; designed to persist to Verify (the single gate). No 'no unverified remain' forbid.
unverified.description: R13 UNVERIFIED FLAG (irreducible floor, KEEP polarity). true = some part of this decision could not be verified by Decide and MUST propagate downstream to Verify. Never forbidden true at this intermediate step.

followup_flows.$comment: Record mode (esp. Review audit flow). dedup by (type, scope_hash) is an M2 validator, not a grammar keyword.
followup_flows.description: Audit-flow followups (Review Decide.Record surfaces review findings here; orchestrator QUEUEs them, does not auto-run). M2 validator: dedup by (type, scope_hash). scope is a RowRef to the finding row.

scope_ref.description: RowRef to the finding/work row this followup addresses (§5 row id, not a file path).

scope_hash.description: Stable hash of the followup scope. M2 validator dedups followup_flows by (type, scope_hash).

options_considered.description: The compared options. Reviewer demands N>=2 (an option set of one is not a plan). Each has an id, approach, trade_offs, and est_effort.

est_effort.description: Relative effort estimate for this option.

verify_probe.$comment: R20 — optional re-runnable assertion attached to an option. Truth is the RE-EXECUTION (M2), not the shape.
verify_probe.description: Optional R20 probe: a command + expected result that would validate this option's premise. M2 validator RE-EXECUTES and compares — self-assertion is not truth.

chosen.description: The selected option + why. option_id MUST reference an id present in options_considered (M2 validator).

sequencing.$comment: Compound sub-flow order / migration cycle order. depends_on values reference step ids within this sequence (M2 acyclicity/existence check).
sequencing.description: Optional priority/dependency ordering (Compound sub-flow order, Migration cycle order). Each entry: a step id + the step ids it depends on. M2 validator: every depends_on id exists and the graph is acyclic.

options_deliberated.description: pyreez deliberation record (option comparison feeding the architecture choice). pyreez is MCP-backed and degradable: when pyreez is unavailable this is the Omitted branch (R22), not an empty array or a fabricated deliberation.

design_document.$comment: Reviewer: design document MUST contain architecture + policy + userflow + req. All four required.
design_document.description: The design document. Reviewer requires all four sub-parts present: architecture, policy(/policies), userflow(s), requirements.

architecture.description: The chosen architecture decision (chosen_architecture in legacy prose).

policies.description: Policy / business rules: [{ rule, scope, exceptions }].

intent_card.$comment: Reviewer: Design must CREATE an emberdeck intent card. emberdeck is MCP-backed and degradable: card creation can fail when ED is unavailable → Omitted branch (R22), not a fake card id.
intent_card.description: The emberdeck intent card produced by this design. emberdeck (ED) is MCP-backed and degradable; when ED is unavailable, this is the Omitted branch (the design still stands but the card was not created), NOT a fabricated card id. Measured.value carries the created card's RowRef-style id.

adr.$comment: R34 — Architecture Decision Record is an M1 FIELD (standalone prose rule deleted; shape forced by grammar). Required in Design.
adr.description: R34 ARCHITECTURE DECISION RECORD (M1 field — R34 standalone prose rule deleted, the shape is now grammar-forced). Records the architectural decision: title, context, decision, consequences, status, optional considered alternatives.

cross_verify_required.$comment: R5 floor — Design mode is HIGH-STAKES, so the cross-verify trigger is carried here. Mitigation, not elimination.
cross_verify_required.description: R5 CROSS-VERIFY TRIGGER (irreducible-floor mitigation). Design mode is high-stakes, so Decide carries the multi-model/pyreez cross-verification trigger. Mitigates self-asserted-truth / semantic-boundary residuals; does NOT eliminate them.

cross_verify_result.description: Optional R5 outcome once cross-verification ran. 'disagree' is the self-misjudgment signal.

task_list.$comment: §10 task model — Design MAY decompose into tasks. R19/R33 ParallelMarker: same-file tasks => parallel_marker=false is an M2 cross-task check, not a grammar keyword. affected count is a CountClaim (R23).
task_list.description: Optional task decomposition for the design (§10 task model). Each task carries R19/R33 parallel_marker (M2 validator: tasks touching the same file MUST both be parallel_marker=false) and an affected_files_count that is a CountClaim (R23 — never a bare integer for a measured count).

parallel_marker.$comment: R19/R33 — same-file tasks => parallel_marker=false is an M2 cross-task check.
parallel_marker.description: R19/R33: may this task run in parallel with siblings. M2 validator: any two tasks sharing a file path MUST both be false (mutual exclusion over file paths).

files.description: File paths this task touches (read input paths, not §5 cross-step refs). Drives the same-file parallel_marker M2 check.

affected_files_count.$comment: R23 — a count of measured things MUST be a CountClaim, never a bare integer.
affected_files_count.description: R23 count-type: number of files this task affects, as a CountClaim {value, source:{command, raw_stdout_sha256}}. M2 validator re-derives value from raw_stdout.

gate_rules.$comment: Compound TOP-LEVEL only. JsonLogic predicate + action enum. Gate executor = orchestrator code (NOT LLM). M2: var paths must exist in sub_flow output structure.
gate_rules.description: Compound top-level ONLY (compound-recursion): gate criteria evaluated by the orchestrator (NOT the LLM) BETWEEN sub-flows. Each rule = a JsonLogic condition predicate + an action enum (proceed|pivot|abort|retry). M2 validator: JsonLogic grammar validity + every `var` path exists in the real sub_flow output structure. pivot cap 2/compound, retry cap 1/sub_flow.

condition.$comment: JsonLogic predicate object (borrowed mini-DSL; no new DSL). Free-form object; M2 validates JsonLogic grammar + var-path existence.
condition.description: A JsonLogic condition predicate ({ operator: [operands...] }). Allowed operators: ==, !=, >, <, >=, <=, and, or, not, in, var. Shape is an open object here; M2 validator checks JsonLogic validity and that every var path resolves in the sub_flow context.

action.description: Gate action taken when condition holds.

sub_flow_sequence.$comment: Compound TOP-LEVEL only. Decide(Design) defines the sub-flow order (decomposition + ordering). Each entry references a sub-flow by RowRef.
sub_flow_sequence.description: Compound top-level ONLY (compound-recursion): the ordered list of sub-flows this Compound decomposes into (Decide-Design owns decomposition + ordering; Triage does NOT). Each entry: a sub-flow ref (RowRef), its flow type, and the prior sub-flow refs it depends on. Reviewer: Compound top-level requires gate_rules + sub_flow_sequence both defined.

sub_flow_ref.description: RowRef to the sub-flow row (§5 row id, never a file path).

depends_on.description: RowRefs of prior sub-flows this one depends on (ordering).

oneOf.$comment: MODE = record. Self-evident decision. decision_record + reason carry the 1-pair the reviewer requires; no plan/design payload. All plan/design-only props forbidden (false) so grammar cannot emit a record carrying them.
MODE = plan. Reviewer: options N>=2 compared + chosen with reason + ordering. Design-only props forbidden.

MODE = design. Full design: design_document(architecture+policy+userflow+req) + intent_card + ADR(R34) + cross_verify(R5) required; optional task_list + Compound gate_rules/sub_flow_sequence. Plan/Record-only props forbidden.
BRANCH B — reclassify_required. Investigate surfaced triage_mismatch; Decide does NOT proceed. Special output. (Triage reclassify cap 3/flow.)

reclassify.description: Reclassify signal: Investigate surfaced a triage_mismatch. Decide produces this special output and does NOT proceed; orchestrator re-enters Triage as a new flow type (reclassify cap 3/flow). Carries the mismatch reason + provenance.

suggested_flow_type.description: Optional advisory new flow type for Triage to consider (advisory only; Triage re-decides).

triage_mismatch_ref.description: RowRef to the Investigate row that surfaced the triage_mismatch (§5 row id).

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH C — request_upstream_deepen. Ground/Investigate too shallow to decide. Signal; orchestrator re-invokes that step with depth=deep (cap 1/flow). Then Verify is the final safety.

deepen_request.description: Upstream deepen signal: Decide cannot decide on shallow Ground/Investigate output. Orchestrator re-invokes the named step with depth=deep (cycle cap 1/flow; if still insufficient, Verify is the final safety with failure_origin=ground|investigate). Carries which step + why + provenance.

target_step.$comment: Which upstream step to deepen. Constrained to the two Decide can deepen (Ground / Investigate) — the shared StepName enum is wider, so this is a tightened local enum.
target_step.description: The upstream step to re-invoke with depth=deep. Only Ground or Investigate (the two Decide rests on).

missing_evidence.description: What specific evidence/facts are missing such that Decide cannot decide.

x-validator-contract.check: M2 REQUIRED: orchestrator computes the FORCED mode from Investigate schema fields (count(compatibility_verdict.issues where severity>=medium)>=2 -> plan; any(risk_surface.severity=high) AND declared=record -> plan; impact_map.affected_files_count>=5 AND declared=record -> plan; architecture_impact.has_architecture_level==true -> design). Assert emitted `mode` == computed forced mode. Mode upgrade triggers evaluated ONLY when compatibility_verdict.result in {proceed, partial_proceed}; otherwise Decide must not have executed at all.
M2: assert depth agrees with mode (record->shallow; plan/design->deep).
M2 (folded into R27): assert declared_next_step == orchestrator-injected expected_next_step. Mismatch = chain divergence.
M2: every mode has decision_record present AND reason grounded in actual Ground/Investigate rows (reason.grounded_in / reason.source_tool resolve to real upstream rows).
M2: options_considered.length >= 2 (already grammar-enforced) AND chosen.option_id references an id present in options_considered.
M2: every depends_on id exists among sequencing[].step and the dependency graph is acyclic.
M2 REQUIRED where present: re-execute verify_probe.command, compare to expected_result. Self-assertion is not truth.
M2 (Design high-stakes): if cross_verify_required=true, the cross-verification (pyreez/multi-model) must actually run; cross_verify_result=disagree is the self-misjudgment signal routed to Verify.
M1 field (shape grammar-forced). No M2 truth check beyond presence in Design mode; ADR content is design prose.
M2 REQUIRED: re-hash CountClaim.source.raw_stdout, confirm raw_stdout_sha256, re-derive value. Bare integer is forbidden by grammar (CountClaim shape).
M2 REQUIRED: any two tasks whose `files` arrays share a path MUST both have parallel_marker=false (mutual exclusion over file paths).
M2: every depends_on id exists among task_list[].id and the graph is acyclic.
M2: dedup by (type, scope_hash); reject duplicate (type, scope_hash) pairs. Followups are QUEUEd by orchestrator, not auto-run.

M2: JsonLogic grammar validity (operators in {==,!=,>,<,>=,<=,and,or,not,in,var}); every `var` path resolves in the real sub_flow output context. Gate executor is orchestrator code, not the LLM. pivot cap 2/compound, retry cap 1/sub_flow.
M2: a Compound TOP-LEVEL Design must define BOTH gate_rules and sub_flow_sequence; sub_flow_sequence depends_on refs must resolve and be acyclic.

Floor (partly M2): every fact/decision/finding item carries a source_tool; provenance presence is grammar-forced, that the named origin truly produced the value is mitigated by cross-verification, not eliminated.
Floor, KEEP polarity: unverified is designed to persist to Verify (the single gate). NO 'no unverified remain' forbid. Whether unverified:false is honest is self-asserted-truth residual.
M2/governor: Triage reclassify cap 3/flow; Decide does not proceed on this branch.
M2/governor: upstream deepen cap 1/flow; after that Verify is the final safety (failure_origin=ground|investigate forces reclassify with depth=deep).
